description: baseline: naive single-threaded, Instant.parse, String.split, ArrayList percentiles
indexOf parsing, manual ISO timestamp, StringBuilder output, double[] sliding state
single sort for both p50/p99 percentiles
1MB reader buffer + sensor ID interning via HashMap
quickselect O(n) for percentiles instead of full sort O(n log n)
fast manual double parser (no improvement, within noise)
compact SensorWindow key (no type string), separate emitted sets, fast double parser
array-indexed windows replacing HashMaps entirely
bounded scan with floor/ceiling in emitReadyWindows
emit every 100K instead of 10K (massive 3.2x improvement)
emit only at end, no intermediate emissions
direct sensor index from digits, eliminates HashMap lookup
memory-mapped I/O with byte-level parsing (no improvement vs BufferedReader)
multi-threaded parallel chunk processing with byte[] parsing
independent file handles per thread (no improvement)
simplified sliding window loop (no improvement)
pre-allocate sensor rows + hoist sRow (no improvement, memory waste)
fixed-offset parsing (JIT already optimizes comma scan)
smaller initial SlidingState array (more resizes, no gain)
parallel merge+emit by sensor range
Arrays.parallelSort slower for ~30K results
BufferedWriter output slower than StringBuilder+print
sorted-order emit but lost parallelism in merge+percentiles
Arrays.sort in-place for percentiles (within noise)
JVM flags -Xmx4g -XX:+UseZGC (slower, ZGC overhead)
sorted SlidingState with O(n+m) merge (no improvement over quickselect)
bounded minute ranges in merge (branch predictor already handles null checks)
bucket-sorted emission + in-place percentiles (no improvement, within noise)
per-minute value storage for sliding windows (concatenation overhead negated savings)
flat-indexed output arrays (cache-unfriendly writes to minute-major layout)
zero-alloc percentiles + direct byte output (byte loop slower than StringBuilder)
double[8] sliding arrays + in-place percentiles (10% worse, too many resizes)
bounded merge ranges + combined merge (code overhead exceeds iteration savings)
single-partition processing (eliminate merge): 38% slower, sequential parsing bottleneck
pre-sorted sliding + merge-sort merge: 13% slower, sort+alloc overhead exceeds quickselect savings
sorted-order emit (no Collections.sort), parallel pct computation (3954132 ev/s, +13.6%)
byte[] output buffer with Long.toString().getBytes(): 10% slower, alloc overhead
parallel emit by minute range + BufferedOutputStream (4327131 ev/s, +9.4%)
optimized sliding window loop: int minute iteration, no modulo, no redundant checks (4504504 ev/s, +4.1%)
fixed-offset comma: CORRECTNESS FAIL, sliding window minute calc wrong
fixed-offset comma (corrected): still CORRECTNESS FAIL, sliding window bug
fixed-offset first comma (Z-aware): no improvement, within noise
parallel byte[] conversion in emit threads (4928536 ev/s, +9.4%)
8MB BufferedOutputStream: 5% slower, cache pressure
cached date computation in timestamp: within noise
fast minute from hour:minute + fixed comma offsets: within noise
FileChannel output: no improvement
skip-ahead newline scan + fixed comma offsets: within noise
pipelined emit: no improvement, already overlapping
direct append (no sb2): within noise
pre-computed timestamp prefixes: within noise (5-run confirmation)
nThreads/2 chunks: no improvement, reduced parse parallelism
bundled emit: pre-computed prefixes + direct append, no sb2 (5010020 ev/s, +1.7%)
bounded minute range: negligible savings (~60 empty slots)
fused merge+emit: 5% worse, poor cross-partition cache locality
data layout transposition [sensor][minute] → [minute][sensor] for cache-friendly parse
SlidingState initial array 64→8: resize overhead > memory savings (-5.2%)
SlidingState initial array 64→16: still regresses (-5%), high GC variance
lazy sliding windows: 1 raw write per event instead of 5, combine at merge time (+2.6%)
